title: UK Government Faces Challenges Over New Social Media Ban for Children
subtitle: As officials work to explain the policy to international partners, questions remain about how the rules will work in practice.

summary.en: The UK government is working to reassure US officials that its new social media ban for children is not an attack on American technology companies. Prime Minister Keir Starmer maintains that the policy is essential for protecting young people from online harm. However, the plan faces criticism from figures like Elon Musk, who claims the law could threaten user privacy. Experts and parents are still waiting for clarity on how the rules will affect popular gaming platforms and educational tools. Further details regarding curfews and age verification methods are expected to be released by ministers in July.
summary.zh: 英國政府正致力於向美國官員保證，其針對兒童的新社群媒體禁令並非針對美國科技公司的攻擊。首相施凱爾堅持認為，該政策對於保護青少年免受網路傷害至關重要。然而，這項計畫面臨馬斯克等人的批評，他聲稱該法律可能威脅使用者隱私。專家與家長仍在等待關於這些規定將如何影響熱門遊戲平台與教育工具的明確說明。關於宵禁與年齡驗證方式的進一步細節，預計將由部長們於七月發布。

paragraphs.en: The UK government is currently working to manage international reactions following its recent announcement of a social media ban for children under 16. Prime Minister Keir Starmer has launched a series of meetings to explain the new policy to the United States government. According to The Guardian, British officials have spent weeks trying to reassure President Trump and his team that these strict rules are intended to protect children rather than target American technology companies.
paragraphs.zh: 英國政府目前正致力於處理國際間對其近期宣布禁止十六歲以下兒童使用社群媒體的反應。首相施凱爾 (Keir Starmer) 已展開一系列會議，向美國政府說明這項新政策。根據《衛報》(The Guardian) 報導，英國官員已花費數週時間試圖向川普 (Donald Trump) 總統及其團隊保證，這些嚴格規定旨在保護兒童，而非針對美國科技公司。

paragraphs.en: This new policy will restrict access to major platforms such as X, Facebook, YouTube, Snapchat, and TikTok. The government claims that these measures are necessary because social media can harm the mental health of young people and make it easier for bullies to harass them. While the Prime Minister previously opposed such a move, he stated that evidence gathered during a 12-week consultation period convinced him that action was required. Despite these efforts to explain the policy, some critics remain vocal. Elon Musk, the owner of X, recently suggested that the law is a way for the government to track citizens, calling it a "wolf in sheep’s clothing."
paragraphs.zh: 這項新政策將限制民眾使用 X、Facebook、YouTube、Snapchat 和 TikTok 等主要平台。政府聲稱這些措施有其必要，因為社群媒體可能損害青少年的心理健康，並讓霸凌者更容易騷擾他們。儘管首相先前曾反對此類舉措，但他表示在為期十二週的諮詢期間所收集的證據，讓他確信必須採取行動。儘管政府努力說明政策，仍有一些批評聲音。X 的擁有者馬斯克 (Elon Musk) 近期暗示，這項法律是政府監控公民的一種手段，並稱其為「披著羊皮的狼」。

paragraphs.en: As the government moves forward, many questions remain about how the ban will be enforced. One major area of uncertainty involves gaming sites like Roblox. While the government has not yet confirmed if these sites will be fully included in the ban, it has announced that all platforms must disable features that allow strangers to send messages to children. According to BBC Technology, experts are watching closely to see if the UK will follow the Australian model, which does not include many gaming sites in its own social media restrictions for young people.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著政府持續推動政策，關於禁令將如何執行仍有許多疑問。其中一個主要的不確定領域涉及像 Roblox 這類遊戲網站。雖然政府尚未確認這些網站是否會完全納入禁令範圍，但已宣布所有平台必須停用允許陌生人向兒童發送訊息的功能。根據 BBC 科技新聞報導，專家正密切關注英國是否會效仿澳洲模式，該模式並未將許多遊戲網站納入其針對青少年的社群媒體限制中。

paragraphs.en: Educational access is another significant concern. YouTube will be included in the ban, but the government has stated that YouTube Kids will remain available. This has raised questions about how students will access educational videos on the main YouTube platform. Google, which owns YouTube, noted that 95% of UK teenagers surveyed said that watching videos helps them with their school work. The government has promised a "narrowly defined list of exemptions" to ensure that educational services remain available to children, but the specific details of these exemptions are not yet clear.
paragraphs.zh: 教育資源的取得是另一個重大隱憂。YouTube 將被納入禁令，但政府表示 YouTube Kids 仍可使用。這引發了關於學生將如何存取 YouTube 主平台上的教育影片之疑慮。擁有 YouTube 的 Google 指出，受訪的英國青少年中有百分之九十五表示，觀看影片有助於他們的課業。政府已承諾會制定一份「定義狹窄的豁免清單」，以確保兒童仍能使用教育服務，但這些豁免的具體細節尚不明確。

paragraphs.en: Beyond the main ban, the government is planning additional restrictions. Ministers are expected to provide an update in July regarding potential late-night curfews for 16- and 17-year-olds and new rules to limit "addictive" features like infinite scrolling and AI chatbots. There is also ongoing discussion about how to handle virtual private networks (VPNs) — tools that hide a user's location online. Some industry leaders, such as the chief executive of Mullvad VPN, suggest that the government might eventually require companies to ask users for identification to prove their age.
paragraphs.zh: 除了主要的禁令外，政府也計畫實施額外的限制。預計部長們將在七月提供關於十六歲與十七歲青少年宵禁的最新消息，以及限制「成癮性」功能（如無限捲動與人工智慧聊天機器人）的新規定。關於如何處理虛擬私人網路 (VPN)（即隱藏使用者線上位置的工具）的討論也在進行中。一些產業領袖，例如 Mullvad VPN 的執行長，認為政府最終可能會要求公司要求使用者提供身分證明以驗證年齡。

paragraphs.en: For many families, the impact of these changes could be significant. Data from Ofcom shows that half of all 13- to 17-year-olds use messaging apps like WhatsApp, which are not currently included in the ban. Meanwhile, among 13- to 15-year-olds, nine out of 10 have a social media account and rely on these platforms as their main source of news. As the government prepares for its next update, the challenge will be balancing the need for child safety with the practical realities of how young people use the internet today.
paragraphs.zh: 對許多家庭而言，這些變化的影響可能相當深遠。英國通訊管理局 (Ofcom) 的數據顯示，所有十三至十七歲的青少年中，有一半使用 WhatsApp 等通訊軟體，而這些軟體目前並未納入禁令。同時，在十三至十五歲的青少年中，有十分之九擁有社群媒體帳號，並將這些平台視為他們獲取新聞的主要來源。隨著政府準備進行下一次更新，挑戰將在於如何在兒童安全需求與青少年現今使用網路的實際情況之間取得平衡。

paragraphs.en: Prime Minister Starmer remains confident that the policy is the right path forward. When asked about the potential for a disagreement with the US administration, he noted that world leaders generally recognize the need to take steps to protect children. As the government continues to "mythbust" in the media and engage with technology companies, the public and parents across the UK will be waiting to see exactly how these rules will change the digital lives of their children in the coming months.
paragraphs.zh: 首相施凱爾仍堅信這項政策是正確的方向。當被問及與美國政府發生分歧的可能性時，他指出世界各國領袖普遍認同有必要採取措施來保護兒童。隨著政府持續在媒體上「破除迷思」並與科技公司接洽，英國各地的民眾與家長將拭目以待，觀察這些規定在未來幾個月內將如何改變他們孩子的數位生活。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Expressing opinions or feelings freely or loudly.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 直言不諱的、大聲疾呼的。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（聲樂），這裡指人勇於表達意見。文中：Despite these efforts to explain the policy, some critics remain vocal.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A person or thing that appears harmless or friendly but is actually dangerous or harmful.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 披著羊皮的狼（外表無害，實則危險）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是英文慣用語，不能字面翻譯。文中：Elon Musk, the owner of X, recently suggested that the law is a way for the government to track citizens, calling it a "wolf in sheep’s clothing."

keyTerms.definitionEn: To prove that a widely held belief or idea is false.
keyTerms.noteZh: 由 myth（迷思）與 bust（打破）組成的動詞，在新聞中常見。文中：As the government continues to "mythbust" in the media and engage with technology companies, the public and parents across the UK will be waiting to see exactly how these rules will change the digital lives of their children in the coming months.

sources.name: BBC Technology
sources.title: Five big questions about the UK's under-16s social media ban

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: UK ministers lobby Trump to avert backlash against social media ban

previousArticles.title: UK Government Announces Social Media Ban for Children Under 16
previousArticles.summaryEn: The UK government has announced a plan to ban children under 16 from major social media platforms by early 2027. Prime Minister Keir Starmer stated the law is necessary to protect young people from bullying and mental health issues. Major tech companies like Meta and YouTube have criticized the move, arguing it could push children toward less safe, unregulated online spaces. Messaging apps and some educational services will remain available to younger users. The government also plans to introduce new restrictions on AI chatbots and features like livestreaming for teenagers.

previousArticles.title: UK Government Announces Social Media Ban for Under-16s
previousArticles.summaryEn: The UK government has announced a plan to ban children under 16 from using major social media platforms. Prime Minister Keir Starmer stated that the law is necessary to protect young people from online harm and bullying. While the ban will not include messaging apps, it will restrict features like livestreaming and contact with strangers. Major technology companies have expressed concern that the ban could push children toward less safe online spaces. The new rules are expected to come into effect in the spring of 2027.

quiz.question: What specific requirement has the UK government announced regarding gaming sites like Roblox?

options.A: They must be completely blocked for all users under 16.
options.B: They must disable features that allow strangers to message children.
options.C: They must provide identification tools to verify user age.
options.D: They are exempt from all current and future government restrictions.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，政府尚未確認是否將遊戲網站完全納入禁令，但明確要求所有平台必須禁用讓陌生人傳送訊息給兒童的功能。選項 A 錯誤，因為禁令範圍尚未定案；選項 C 是關於 VPN 的討論，並非針對遊戲網站的直接要求；選項 D 錯誤，因為政府並未給予遊戲網站完全豁免權。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the UK government's approach to educational content on YouTube?

options.A: The government plans to ban YouTube Kids to ensure total control.
options.B: The government believes students do not actually use YouTube for school work.
options.C: The government is struggling to balance safety bans with the need for educational access.
options.D: The government has already finalized a list of specific educational channels that are allowed.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第四段提到 YouTube 主平台將被禁，但 YouTube Kids 會保留，且政府承諾會有「狹義定義的豁免清單」。這顯示政府在執行保護兒童的禁令時，必須同時處理學生依賴 YouTube 學習的現實需求，兩者之間存在平衡上的困難。選項 A 與文意相反；選項 B 與文中提到的 Google 調查數據衝突；選項 D 錯誤，因為文中明確指出具體細節尚未明朗。

quiz.question: In the second paragraph, what does the phrase 'wolf in sheep’s clothing' imply about Elon Musk's view of the law?

options.A: He believes the law is harmless but poorly designed.
options.B: He thinks the law hides a dangerous or deceptive agenda behind a friendly appearance.
options.C: He feels the law is too weak to actually protect children as intended.
options.D: He believes the government is being manipulated by technology companies.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。這是一個慣用語，指表面上看起來無害或善良（保護兒童），但實際上隱藏著惡意或危險的意圖（監控公民）。Elon Musk 用此形容該法律，暗示他認為政府聲稱的保護動機是虛假的。選項 A 與慣用語意義相反；選項 C 錯誤，因為他認為這法律不僅僅是無效，而是具備威脅性；選項 D 則與他對政府動機的質疑不符。

quiz.question: What is the primary focus of the article?

options.A: A detailed history of how social media companies have failed to protect children.
options.B: The diplomatic tension between the UK and the US regarding technology regulations.
options.C: The challenges and complexities the UK faces in implementing its new social media ban for minors.
options.D: A comprehensive guide for parents on how to navigate the upcoming internet restrictions.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文從政策宣布開始，討論了對美國的溝通、執行細節的模糊地帶（如遊戲網站、教育需求）、未來潛在的規範（如宵禁、VPN），以及對家庭的影響。文章旨在呈現這項政策在落實過程中面臨的種種挑戰與不確定性。選項 A 僅是背景的一部分；選項 B 僅在開頭提到；選項 D 不是文章的主旨，文章並非提供給家長的指南。
